evidence_quotes.quote: There are alternatives to just shutting down central servers, without which games are unplayable.
In many cases the same companies requires us gamers to log to internet, to play a single-player game, which could have been played without internet connection.
convoluted Terms and Conditions we are forced as well to agree to.

paragraphs.text: I want to express my support to Stop Killing Games initiative. Too many games we have lost to corporate greed and bad will. There are alternatives to just shutting down central servers, without which games are unplayable. In many cases the same companies requires us gamers to log to internet, to play a single-player game, which could have been played without internet connection. And when a game is unprofitable, they shut down the servers. EU citizens who have bough the game at a full price are unable to use that product anymore. Many times the internet lock-in can be lifted, when the game is about to be delete. It's a lack of good will, it doesn't even cost them anything, there are people who will do for free to save their favorite games on their own if only let to by corporate greed and bad will dressed as convoluted Terms and Conditions we are forced as well to agree to.
